{"step_by_step_thinking": "Hemochromatosis type 4, also known as ferroportin disease, is caused by mutations in the SLC40A1 gene. According to the documents, type 4 hemochromatosis is inherited in an autosomal dominant fashion. This means that only one copy of the mutated gene is sufficient to cause the disorder, which is characteristic of dominant inheritance, not recessive inheritance. Recessive inheritance requires two copies of the mutated gene (homozygous or compound heterozygous) to manifest the disease. The documents explicitly state that 'Type 4 HH is the only known form of hemochromatosis that can be inherited in an autosomal dominant fashion' and that 'the ferroportin mutations are dominant.' Therefore, it is not caused by a mutation in a recessive gene.", "answer_choice": "B"}
